assertions.text: Vehicle speed was approximately 105 to 110 km/h at the time of the impact.
The vehicle experienced one strong blow (a single impact).
The car remained drivable after the impact and slowed steadily over the following seconds.
The impact was strong but did not render the car undrivable (damage was not severe enough to immobilize the vehicle).
The strong blow occurred while traveling at about 105–110 km/h, and the car slowed steadily after that.

verdicts.evidence: The GPS-derived speed at t=9–10 s (around impact) is [104.0 km/h at t=9.0–10.0 s](T3) and the per-sample GPS shows [104.0 km/h at t=9.0 and 104.0 km/h at t=10.0](T4), which is within ~15% of the claimed [105–110 km/h] (compare to initial [110.0 km/h at t=0.0](T2)).

Detect_events found a single dominant impact spike on accel_x at [peak_t=9.97 s magnitude=5.51 g](T3) and additional smaller simultaneous spikes on accel_y and accel_z at [peak_t=10.0 s magnitudes 2.65 g and 3.72 g respectively](T3), consistent with one strong collision event occurring once around t≈10 s. The event list shows one primary impact spike window centered at [9.97–10.0 s](T3).

GPS speed shows a steady decline after the impact: from [104.0 km/h at t=10.0 s] to [92.0 km/h at t=11.0 s] to [87.0 km/h at t=12.0 s] and continuing down to [44.0 km/h at t=15.0 s and 27.0 km/h at t=16.0 s](T4). Detect_events flagged speed drops across successive intervals ([104→87 km/h between t=9–12 s], [92→67 km/h between t=11–14 s], [78→44 km/h between t=13–15 s]) showing sustained slowing after the impact (T3).

Telemetry shows a large impact spike (accel_x peak [−5.506 g at t=9.97 s](T2)) and an integrated device-frame delta‑v on accel_x of [−53.8 km/h over the window](T2), but device-frame axes and vehicle immobilization are not directly measured by these channels; whether damage rendered the car undrivable cannot be confirmed by sensors alone and is therefore unverifiable.

The timing and speed match: a strong impact spike occurs at [~9.97–10.0 s](T3) while GPS shows the vehicle speed at that time was [104.0 km/h at t=9–10 s](T4), and the GPS speed then declines steadily to [27.0 km/h by t=16.0 s](T2/T4), matching the sequence described.

result.rationale: Sensor data show a single strong impact near t≈9.97–10.0 s while traveling at ~104 km/h, followed by sustained speed reductions; immobilization cannot be assessed from telemetry alone.

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_z.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept
